translations.source: Smallpox Overview: Smallpox is an acute contagious disease caused by the variola virus, a member of the orthopoxvirus family. It was one of the most devastating diseases known to humanity and caused millions of deaths before it was eradicated. It is believed to have existed for at least 3000 years. The smallpox vaccine, created by Edward Jenner in 1796, was the first successful vaccine to be developed. He observed that milkmaids who previously had caught cowpox did not catch smallpox and showed that a similar inoculation could be used to prevent smallpox in other people. The World Health Organization launched an intensified plan to eradicate smallpox in 1967. Widespread immunization and surveillance were conducted around the world for several years. The last known natural case was in Somalia in 1977. In 1980 WHO declared smallpox eradicated – the only infectious disease to achieve this distinction. This remains among the most notable and profound public health successes in history. Symptoms: Early symptoms of smallpox include high fever, fatigue and severe back pain, and less often, abdominal pain and vomiting. Two to 3 days later the virus produces a characteristic rash with bumps full of a clear liquid, which later fill with pus and finally develop a crust that dries and falls off. The rash begins on the face and hands, then spreads to the rest of the body. Lesions develop in the mucous membranes of the nose and mouth and ulcerate soon after formation. Smallpox is transmitted from person to person via infective droplets during close contact with infected people who have symptoms of the disease, or in some cases through contaminated clothing and bedding. It has an incubation period of 7–17 days after exposure and only becomes infectious once a fever develops. People remain infectious until the last scabs fall off. Smallpox was fatal in up to 30% of cases.WHO smallpox recognition card WHO response: The period since eradication has been defined by a lengthy and complex debate focussed on the destruction of the last remaining stocks of live variola virus. In 1996, at the 49th World Health Assembly, Member States decided to have this stock destroyed in June 1999, and only to allow small samples to be kept for research purposes at two designated secure laboratories – one in the United States and one in the Russian Federation. Temporary retention of the existing stocks of variola virus are used for further essential research, which is overseen by the Advisory Committee for Variola Virus Research. This group meets on an annual basis. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat based in WHO Headquarters coordinates all smallpox eradication-related and post-eradication activities. These include overseeing permitted research with live variola virus for the development of countermeasures such as vaccine and antivirals.
translations.reference: Ndui Muhtasari: Ndui ni ugonjwa wa kuambukiza kwa papo hapo unaosababishwa na virusi vya variola, mwanachama wa familia ya orthopoxvirus. Ilikuwa ni moja ya magonjwa mabaya zaidi yanayojulikana kwa wanadamu na kusababisha mamilioni ya vifo kabla ya kutokomezwa. Inaaminika kuwa imekuwepo kwa angalau miaka 3000. Chanjo ya ndui, iliyoundwa na Edward Jenner mnamo 1796, ilikuwa chanjo ya kwanza iliyofanikiwa kutengenezwa. Aliona kuwa wahudumu wa maziwa ambao hapo awali walishika ndui hawakupata ugonjwa wa ndui na alionyesha kuwa chanjo kama hiyo inaweza kutumika kuzuia ugonjwa wa ndui kwa watu wengine. Shirika la Afya Ulimwenguni lilizindua mpango ulioimarishwa wa kutokomeza ugonjwa wa ndui mnamo 1967. Chanjo iliyoenea na ufuatiliaji ulifanyika duniani kote kwa miaka kadhaa. Kisa cha mwisho kilichojulikana kilikuwa Somalia mnamo 1977. Mnamo 1980 WHO ilitangaza kutokomeza ugonjwa wa ndui - ugonjwa pekee wa kuambukiza kufikia tofauti hii. Hii inasalia kuwa miongoni mwa mafanikio mashuhuri na makubwa ya afya ya umma katika historia. Dalili: Dalili za awali za ndui ni pamoja na homa kali, uchovu na maumivu makali ya mgongo, na mara chache zaidi, maumivu ya tumbo na kutapika. Siku mbili hadi 3 baadaye virusi hutoa upele wenye tabia ya matuta yaliyojaa kioevu wazi, ambayo baadaye hujaa na usaha na hatimaye kuendeleza ukoko ambao hukauka na kumomonyoka. Upele huanza kwenye uso na mikono, kisha huenea kwa mwili wote. Vidonda vinakua kwenye utando wa mucous wa pua na mdomo na vidonda mara baada ya kuundwa. Ndui huambukizwa kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu kupitia matone ya kuambukiza wakati wa kuwasiliana kwa karibu na watu walioambukizwa ambao wana dalili za ugonjwa huo, au katika visa vingine kupitia nguo zilizochafuliwa na matandiko. Ina kipindi cha incubation cha siku 7-17 baada ya kuambukizwa na huambukiza mara tu homa inapoanza. Watu hubakia kuambukizwa hadi vipele vya mwisho vitoke. Ndui iliua hadi asilimia 30 ya visa.Kadi ya utambuzi wa ndui ya WHO Jibu la WHO: Kipindi tangu kutokomezwa kimefafanuliwa na mjadala mrefu na tata unaolenga uharibifu wa hifadhi ya mwisho iliyobaki ya virusi vya variola hai. Mnamo 1996, katika Mkutano wa 49 wa Afya Ulimwenguni, Nchi Wanachama ziliamua kuharibu hisa mnamo Juni 1999, na kuruhusu tu sampuli ndogo kuhifadhiwa kwa madhumuni ya utafiti katika maabara mbili zilizowekwa salama - moja nchini Merika na moja katika Urusi. Uhifadhi wa muda wa hifadhi zilizopo za virusi vya variola hutumiwa kwa utafiti zaidi muhimu, ambao unasimamiwa na Kamati ya Ushauri ya Utafiti wa Virusi vya Variola. Kikundi hiki hukutana kila mwaka. Sekretarieti ya Ugonjwa wa Ndui ya WHO iliyoko Makao Makuu ya WHO inaratibu shughuli zote zinazohusiana na kutokomeza ndui na baada ya kutokomeza. Hizi ni pamoja na kusimamia utafiti unaoidhinishwa na virusi vya variola hai kwa ajili ya kutengeneza hatua za kupinga kama vile chanjo na dawa za kuzuia virusi.

translations.source: Since the eradication era, safer vaccines and specific treatments have been developed for smallpox and related diseases such as monkeypox. The Secretariat is also responsible for emergency preparedness and manages a smallpox vaccine emergency stockpile in the unlikely event that the disease re-emerges. The Secretariat manages the biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the authorized variola virus repositories as mandated by the World Health Assembly, and ensures annual reporting to WHO Governing Bodies. The Smallpox Secretariat supports WHO Archives in preserving the global history of smallpox and documenting the lessons learned from successful eradication of smallpox. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat's current roles and responsibilities include: Variola virus research and development of countermeasures organizing annual meetings of the WHO Advisory Committee on Variola Virus Research (ACVVR) coordinating review of research proposals for work with variola virus through the ACVVR managing relationships with the two variola virus repositories (which are also WHO Collaborating Centres) recording, processing and monitoring requests from researchers for variola virus DNA from the 2 repositories under specific rules conducting biennial biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the 2 global variola virus repositories developing normative guidance regarding research on variola virus DNA and related matters Emergency preparedness and response coordinating and preparing normative guidance on development and use of medical countermeasures for orthopoxviruses developing preparedness and response strategies, and tools to implement preparedness measures maintaining and managing the smallpox global vaccine stockpile coordinating development of a laboratory network for orthopoxvirus detection and diagnostics developing normative guidance and response strategies for human monkeypox and other orthopoxviruses supporting capacity-building and developing training materials contributing to global health security initiatives Preserving global smallpox history overseeing the Smallpox Eradication Programme archives in collaboration with WHO Archives and the WHO Library leading on policy discussions and commemorative events regarding smallpox eradication reporting annually to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly The Secretariat also mobilizes and manages funding for these activities and for ad hoc events as required such as technical consultations and training.
translations.reference: Tangu enzi ya kutokomeza, chanjo salama na matibabu mahususi yametengenezwa kwa ugonjwa wa ndui na magonjwa yanayohusiana kama vile ndui ya nyani. Sekretarieti pia inawajibika kwa maandalizi ya dharura na inasimamia hifadhi ya dharura ya chanjo ya ndui katika tukio lisilowezekana kwamba ugonjwa huo utaibuka tena. Sekretarieti inasimamia ukaguzi wa usalama wa viumbe na usalama wa viumbe hai wa hifadhi zilizoidhinishwa za virusi vya variola kama ilivyoagizwa na Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni, na kuhakikisha ripoti ya kila mwaka kwa Mashirika Yanayoongoza ya WHO. Sekretarieti ya Ndui inaunga mkono Hifadhi za WHO katika kuhifadhi historia ya kimataifa ya ndui na kuweka kumbukumbu za mafunzo tuliyopata kutokana na kutokomeza kabisa ugonjwa wa ndui. Majukumu ya sasa ya Sekretarieti na wajibu wa Ndui kwa WHO ni pamoja na: Utafiti wa virusi vya Variola na maendeleo ya hatua za kupinga kuandaa mikutano ya kila mwaka ya Kamati ya Ushauri ya WHO kuhusu Utafiti wa Virusi vya Variola (ACVVR) kuratibu mapitio ya mapendekezo ya utafiti wa kufanya kazi na virusi vya variola kupitia ACVVR kusimamia mahusiano hazina na virusi viwili vya variola (ambazo pia ni Vituo vya Kushirikiana vya WHO) kurekodi, kuchakata na kufuatilia maombi kutoka kwa watafiti wa DNA ya virusi vya variola kutoka kwa hazina 2 chini ya sheria maalum. kufanya ukaguzi wa usalama wa viumbe na ulinzi wa viumbe hifadhi 2 za kimataifa katika hazina za virusi vya variola kuendeleza mwongozo wa kawaida kuhusu utafiti juu ya DNA ya virusi vya variola na masuala yanayohusiana Maandalizi ya dharura na majibu kuratibu na kuandaa mwongozo wa kawaida juu ya ukuzaji na utumiaji wa hatua za matibabu kwa virusi vya orthopox. kuandaa mikakati ya kujiandaa na kukabiliana, na zana za kutekeleza hatua za kujitayarisha kudumisha na kusimamia hifadhi ya kimataifa ya chanjo ya ndui kuratibu maendeleo ya mtandao wa maabara kwa utambuzi na uchunguzi wa virusi vya orthopox kuunda mwongozo wa kawaida na mikakati ya kukabiliana na ndui ya nyani kwa binadamu na virusi vingine vya (ndui) orthopox kusaidia kujenga uwezo na kuendeleza nyenzo za mafunzo kuchangia mipango ya usalama wa afya duniani Kuhifadhi historia ya ndui duniani kusimamia kumbukumbu za Mpango wa Kutokomeza Ugonjwa wa Ndui kwa ushirikiano na Shirika la Kumbukumbu la WHO na Maktaba ya WHO kuongoza mijadala ya sera na matukio ya ukumbusho kuhusu kutokomeza ugonjwa wa ndui kuripoti kila mwaka kwa Bodi ya Utendaji ya WHO na Bunge la Afya Ulimwenguni Sekretarieti pia hukusanya na kusimamia ufadhili wa shughuli hizi na kwa matukio ya dharura kama inavyohitajika kama vile mashauriano ya kiufundi na mafunzo.

translations.source: Snakebite envenoming Overview: Snakebite envenoming is a potentially life-threatening disease caused by toxins in the bite of a venomous snake. Envenoming can also be caused by having venom sprayed into the eyes by certain species of snakes that have the ability to spit venom as a defence measure. Inadequate past efforts to control snakebite envenoming has produced fragmented, inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not attend health centres or hospitals and instead rely on traditional treatments. However, available data show 4.5–5.4 million people get bitten by snakes annually. Of this, 1.8–2.7 million develop clinical illness and 81 000 to 138 000 die from complications. High-risk groups include rural agricultural workers, herders, fishermen, hunters, working children, people living in poorly constructed houses and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Morbidity and mortality occur most frequently among young people and children suffer higher case fatality. Furthermore, women experience increased barriers to accessing medical care in some cultures and pregnant women are extremely vulnerable. An ongoing crisis restricting access to safe, effective antivenom treatment in many regions, and particularly sub-Saharan Africa, is one factor that contributes to the predisposition for seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Bites or sprayed venom from venomous snakes can cause a range of acute and serious medical emergencies. Envenoming from different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some more serious than others. This makes the preparation of correct antivenoms an ongoing problem. Envenoming can cause severe paralysis that may prevent breathing, making immediate medical attention critical. People may also experience bleeding disorders that can lead to fatal haemorrhages or irreversible kidney failure. Severe local tissue destruction may also occur, which can lead to permanent disability and even limb amputation. Children are at higher risk of severe effects due to lower body mass than adults. Most deaths and serious consequences from snake bites are entirely preventable by making safe and effective antivenoms more widely available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High quality snake antivenoms are the only effective treatment to prevent or reverse most of the venomous effects of snake bites. Treatment: Snake antivenoms are effective treatments to prevent or reverse most of the harmful effects of snakebite envenoming and are included in the WHO list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, along with raising awareness on primary prevention methods among communities and health workers, are the best ways to limit serious consequences and deaths from snakebite envenoming. After a bite by a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove anything tight from around the bitten part of the body to avoid harm if swelling occurs.
translations.reference: Kung'atwa na nyoka Muhtasari: Kuumwa na nyoka ni ugonjwa unaoweza kuhatarisha maisha unaosababishwa na sumu wakati wa kuumwa na nyoka mwenye sumu. Uchafuzi unaweza pia kusababishwa na kunyunyiziwa sumu machoni na aina fulani za nyoka ambao wana uwezo wa kutema sumu kama njia ya kujikinga. Juhudi duni za hapo awali za kudhibiti kuumwa kwa nyoka zimetoa data iliyogawanyika, isiyo sahihi ya epidemiolojia. Waathiriwa wengi hawahudhurii vituo vya afya au hospitali na badala yake wanategemea matibabu ya kienyeji. Hata hivyo, takwimu zinazopatikana zinaonyesha watu milioni 4.5–5.4 huumwa na nyoka kila mwaka. Kati ya hizi, milioni 1.8-2.7 hupata ugonjwa wa kliniki na 81,000 hadi 138,000 hufa kutokana na matatizo. Vikundi vilivyo katika hatari kubwa ni pamoja na wafanyikazi wa kilimo vijijini, wafugaji, wavuvi, wawindaji, watoto wanaofanya kazi, watu wanaoishi katika nyumba ambazo hazijajengwa vizuri na wale ambao wana fursa ndogo ya kupata elimu na huduma za afya. Maradhi na vifo hutokea mara nyingi zaidi miongoni mwa vijana na watoto hukumbwa na vifo vingi zaidi. Zaidi ya hayo, wanawake hupata vikwazo vilivyoongezeka vya kupata huduma za matibabu katika baadhi ya tamaduni na wanawake wajawazito wako katika hatari kubwa. Mgogoro unaoendelea unaozuia upatikanaji wa matibabu salama na madhubuti ya antivenom katika maeneo mengi, na hasa Afrika Kusini mwa Jangwa la Sahara, ni sababu moja inayochangia uwezekano wa kutafuta msaada kupitia dawa za jadi. Dalili: Kuumwa au kunyunyiziwa sumu kutoka kwa nyoka wenye sumu kunaweza kusababisha aina mbalimbali za dharura za kimatibabu. Ugonjwa wa sumu kutoka kwa aina tofauti za nyoka unaweza kusababisha dalili tofauti, zingine mbaya zaidi kuliko zingine. Hii inafanya utayarishaji wa antivenino sahihi kuwa tatizo linaloendelea. Evenoming inaweza kusababisha kupooza hatari ambayo inaweza kuzuia kupumua, na kufanya matibabu ya haraka muhimu. Watu wanaweza pia kupata matatizo ya kutokwa na damu ambayo yanaweza kusababisha kuvuja damu mbaya au kushindwa kwa figo kusikoweza kurekebishwa. Uharibifu mkubwa wa tishu za ndani pia unaweza kutokea, ambayo inaweza kusababisha ulemavu wa kudumu na hata kukatwa kwa kiungo. Watoto wako katika hatari kubwa ya athari kali kutokana na uzito mdogo wa mwili kuliko watu wazima. Vifo vingi na madhara makubwa kutokana na kuumwa na nyoka vinaweza kuzuilika kabisa kwa kufanya antivenom salama na madhubuti zipatikane na kufikiwa zaidi, hasa katika maeneo yenye hatari kubwa. Dawa za kupambana na nyoka za ubora wa juu ndizo matibabu pekee madhubuti ya kuzuia au kubadilisha athari nyingi za sumu za kuumwa na nyoka. Matibabu: Dawa za kuua nyoka ni matibabu madhubuti ya kuzuia au kubadilisha athari nyingi za kuumwa na nyoka na zimejumuishwa katika orodha ya WHO ya dawa muhimu. Upatikanaji na ufikiwaji wa dawa hizi, pamoja na kuongeza uelewa juu ya mbinu za kimsingi za kuzuia miongoni mwa jamii na wahudumu wa afya, ni njia bora za kupunguza madhara makubwa na vifo kutokana na kuumwa na nyoka. Baada ya kuumwa na nyoka anayeshukiwa kuwa na sumu, fuata hatua hizi: Ondoka mara moja kutoka eneo ambalo kuumwa kulitokea. Ondoa kitu chochote kinachokaza karibu na sehemu ya mwili iliyoumwa ili kuepusha madhara ikiwa uvimbe utatokea.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided text into Swahili.gs, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the target audience and the nature of the document (medical/health information). I've included explanations of some choices after the translation to justify certain phrasing.  I've also tried to adhere to the Swahili.gs orthography.

**Snakebite Envenoming (Usumbufu wa Sumu ya Nyoka) - Jinsi ya Kujihifadhi na Matibabu**

**Ufafanuzi:** Usumbufu wa sumu ya nyoka ni ugonjwa unaoweza kutatiza maisha, unaotokana na sumu iliyo ndani ya ncha ya nyoka yenye uwezo wa kutooa sumu. Usumbufu huu pia unaweza kutokea ikiwa sumu inatiririka kwenye macho kwa nyoka fulani wanaoweza kutumia sumu yao kujilinda.  Juhudi za awali za kudhibiti usumbufu wa sumu ya nyoka hazijakamilika, na zimetuletea taarifa zisizo sahihi kuhusu jinsi ya kuhesabu idadi ya wagonjwa. Watu wengi wanaoathirika hawaendi vituo vya afya au hospitali, badala yake huwatumikia tiba za jadi. Hata hivyo, taarifa zilizopatikana zinaonyesha kuwa watu milioni 4.5 hadi 5.4 huugua kutokana na kuumwa na nyoka kila mwaka. Kati ya hayo, milioni 1.8 hadi 2.7 huugua ugonjwa mbaya, na kati ya wagonjwa hao, kati ya 81,000 hadi 138,000 huishi. Vikundi vinavyokumbwa zaidi ni wafanyakazi wa shamba, wafugaji, wavuvi, wawindaji, watoto wanaofanya kazi, watu wanaoishi nyumba zisizojengwa vizuri, na wale wenye upungufu wa elimu na huduma za afya. Ugonjwa na vifo hutokea mara nyingi miongoni mwa vijana, na watoto huathirika zaidi. Pia, wanawake wanakumbwa na kikwazo cha kupata huduma za matibabu katika baadhi ya tamaduni, na wanawake wajawazito wako hatarini sana.  Ukosefu wa tiba ya sumu ya nyoka (antivenom) salama na yenye ufanisi katika maeneo mengi, hasa Afrika ya Mashariki na Kusini, ni miongoni mwa sababu zinazochangia watu kutafuta tiba za jadi.

**Dalili:**  Kuuma au sumu iliyoingia kwenye macho kutoka kwa nyoka wenye uwezo wa kutooa sumu inaweza kusababisha matatizo mbalimbali ya afya makubwa na ya haraka. Usumbufu wa sumu kutokana na nyoka tofauti unaweza kusababisha dalili tofauti, ambazo zinaweza kuwa kali zaidi kuliko zingine. Hii inafanya iwe vigumu kutayarisha tiba ya sumu ya nyoka inayofaa. Usumbufu wa sumu unaweza kusababisha udhaifu mkuu unaoweza kuzuia mtu kupumua, hivyo ni muhimu kupata matibabu mara moja.  Mtu anaweza pia kupata matatizo ya damu ambayo yanaweza kusababisha damu nyingi au kushindwa kwa figo isiyoweza kurekebishwa.  Uharibifu mkuu wa tishu pia unaweza kutokea, na kusababisha ulemavu wa kudumu au hata kukatwa kwa mguu. Watoto huathirika zaidi kwa sababu ya uzito wao mdogo kuliko watu wazima. Vifo vingi na matokeo mabaya ya kuumwa na nyoka yanaweza kuzuiwa kwa kutengeneza tiba ya sumu ya nyoka salama na yenye ufanisi na kuipatia watu wengi, hasa katika maeneo yenye hatari kubwa. Tiba ya sumu ya nyoka ni tiba pekee yenye ufanisi ya kuzuia au kuondoa madhara mengi ya sumu ya nyoka.

**Matibabu:** Tiba ya sumu ya nyoka ni matibabu yenye ufanisi ya kuzuia au kuondoa madhara ya usumbufu wa sumu ya nyoka, na imejumuishwa katika orodha ya dawa muhimu za Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO).  Upatikanaji na uwezekano wa tiba hizi, pamoja na kuongeza uelewa kuhusu jinsi ya kujilinda miongoni mwa jamii na wataalamu wa afya, ndio njia nzuri zaidi za kuzuia matokeo mabaya na vifo kutokana na usumbufu wa sumu ya nyoka.  Baada ya kuumwa na nyoka anayeaminika kuwa na sumu, fuata hatua hizi:  Haraka, ondoka katika eneo ambapo uliumwa. Ondoa vitu vyovyote ambavyo vinafunga eneo lililo uumwa ili kuepuka uvimbe.

---

**Explanation of Choices & Swahili.gs Considerations:**

*   **"Usumbufu wa Sumu ya Nyoka"**: This is a direct translation of "Snakebite Envenoming" and is a commonly used term.
*   **"Tiba za Jadi"**:  This is the standard Swahili term for "traditional treatments."
*   **Numbers:** I've used Arabic numerals as is standard.
*   **Clarity over Literal Translation:** In some cases, I prioritized clarity for a Swahili speaker over a word-for-word translation. For example, rephrasing sentences to flow more naturally.
*   **WHO**: I kept the acronym WHO as it is widely recognized.
*   **Swahili.gs Orthography:** I've tried to adhere to the Swahili.gs orthography, which emphasizes consistent vowel representation and avoids some of the variations found in other Swahili writing systems.
*   **Emphasis on Prevention:** I've highlighted the importance of prevention and access to antivenom throughout the translation.
*   **"Hatari Sana"**: Used to emphasize the vulnerability of pregnant women.

**Important Notes:**

*   **Context is Key:**  This translation is based on the provided text.  The best translation always depends on the specific context and intended audience.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  It is *highly recommended* that a native Swahili speaker (preferably one with medical knowledge) review this translation for accuracy and cultural appropriateness before it is used in any official capacity.
*   **Terminology:** Medical terminology can be complex.  Confirming the correct Swahili terms with medical professionals is crucial.

translations.source: Reassure the victim, as most venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death. Immobilize the person completely and transport the person to a health facility as soon as possible Applying pressure at the bite site with a pressure pad may be suitable in some cases. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal medicines. Paracetamol may be given for local pain (which can be severe). Vomiting may occur, so place the person on their left side in the recovery position. Closely monitor airway and breathing and be ready to resuscitate if necessary
translations.reference: Mhakikishie mhathiriwa, kwani kuumwa na nyoka wengi wenye sumu hakusababishi kifo cha papo hapo. Zuisha mtu huyo kabisa na umpeleke kwenye kituo cha afya haraka iwezekanavyo Kuweka shinikizo kwenye sehemu za kung'atwa na padi ya shinikizo inayoweza kufaa katika baadhi ya matukio. Epuka njia za jadi za misaada ya kwanza au dawa za mitishamba. Paracetamol inaweza kutolewa kwa maumivu ya ndani (ambayo inaweza kuwa kali). Kutapika kunaweza kutokea, hivyo kumweka mtu upande wake wa kushoto katika nafasi ya kurejeshwa. Fuatilia kwa karibu njia ya hewa na kupumua na uwe tayari kufufua ikiwa ni lazima
translations.hypothesis: Hakikisha unampa msaada wa kisaikolojia mtu aliyeumwa na nyoka sumu, kwani mtego mwingi wa nyoka sumu hausababishi kifo papo hapo. Hakikisha mtu huyo hakufanyi tendo lolote na usafirishe kwenda kituo cha afya haraka iwezekanavyo. Kuweka shinikizo mahali alipoumwa kwa kitambaa kinachofinya inaweza kufaa katika hali fulani. Epuka mbinu za kwanza za kusaidia au dawa za asili. Paracetamol inaweza kutolewa kwa maumivu ya eneo hilo (ambayo yanaweza kuwa makali). Kutapika kunaweza kutokea, kwa hivyo weka mtu huyo pembe ya kushoto katika nafasi ya uponuzi. Fuatilia njia ya hewa na kupumua kwa karibu na uwe tayari kuifufua ikiwa ni lazima.

translations.source: Soil-transmitted helminthiases Overview: Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common infections worldwide and affect the poorest and most deprived communities. They are transmitted by eggs present in human faeces which in turn contaminate soil in areas where sanitation is poor. The main species that infect people are the roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), the whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) and the hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms produce a wide range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea, abdominal pain), general malaise and weakness. Hookworms cause chronic intestinal blood loss that result in anaemia. Soil-transmitted helminths are transmitted by eggs that are passed in the faeces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestine where they produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas that lack adequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: eggs that are attached to vegetables are ingested when the vegetables are not carefully cooked, washed or peeled; eggs are ingested from contaminated water sources; eggs are ingested by children who play in the contaminated soil and then put their hands in their mouths without washing them. Symptoms: Morbidity is related to the number of worms harboured. People with infections of light intensity (few worms) usually do not suffer from the infection. Heavier infections can cause a range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, and impaired growth and physical development. Infections of very high intensity can cause intestinal obstruction that should be treated surgically. S. stercoralis may cause dermatological and gastro-intestinal morbidity and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In case of reduced host immunity, the parasite can cause the hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome that is invariably fatal if not promptly and properly cured and is often fatal despite the treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously endorsed a resolution (WHA54.19) urging endemic countries to start seriously tackling worms, specifically schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminths. The strategy for control of soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control morbidity through the periodic treatment of at-risk people living in endemic areas. People at risk are: preschool children school-age children women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters and breastfeeding women) adults in certain high-risk occupations such as tea-pickers or miners.
translations.reference: helminthiases ya udongo Muhtasari: Maambukizi ya ugonjwa wa helminth (STH) ni miongoni mwa maambukizi ya kawaida duniani kote na huathiri jamii maskini zaidi na maskini zaidi. Husambazwa na mayai yaliyopo kwenye kinyesi cha binadamu ambayo nayo huchafua udongo katika maeneo ambayo usafi wa mazingira ni duni. Aina kuu zinazoambukiza watu ni minyoo (Ascaris lumbricoides), mjeledi (Trichuris trichiura) na ndoano (Necator americanus na Ancylostoma duodenale). Minyoo ya matumbo hutoa dalili nyingi ikiwa ni pamoja na udhihirisho wa matumbo (kuhara, maumivu ya tumbo), malaise ya jumla na udhaifu. Hookworms husababisha kupoteza damu kwa muda mrefu kwa utumbo na kusababisha anemia. Helminths ya udongo hupitishwa kwa mayai ambayo hupitia kwenye kinyesi cha watu walioambukizwa. Minyoo ya watu wazima huishi kwenye utumbo ambapo hutoa maelfu ya mayai kila siku. Katika maeneo ambayo hayana usafi wa kutosha, mayai haya huchafua udongo.: mayai ambayo yameshikanishwa na mboga humezwa wakati mboga haijapikwa kwa uangalifu, kuosha au kuchambuliwa; mayai humezwa kutoka kwa vyanzo vya maji vilivyochafuliwa; mayai humezwa na watoto wanaocheza kwenye udongo uliochafuliwa na kisha kuweka mikono midomoni mwao bila kunawa. Dalili: Ugonjwa unahusiana na idadi ya minyoo waliohifadhiwa. Watu walio na maambukizi ya nguvu ya mwanga (minyoo wachache) kwa kawaida hawateseki kutokana na maambukizi. Maambukizi mazito zaidi yanaweza kusababisha dalili mbalimbali ikiwa ni pamoja na udhihirisho wa matumbo (kuhara na maumivu ya tumbo), utapiamlo, malaise ya jumla na udhaifu, na kuharibika kwa ukuaji na ukuaji wa mwili. Maambukizi ya nguvu ya juu sana yanaweza kusababisha kizuizi cha matumbo ambacho kinapaswa kutibiwa kwa upasuaji. S. stercoralis inaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa ngozi na utumbo na pia inajulikana kuhusishwa na utapiamlo sugu kwa watoto. Katika kesi ya kupungua kwa kinga ya mwenyeji, vimelea vinaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa hyperinfection/usambazaji ambao ni hatari kila wakati ikiwa haujaponywa mara moja na ipasavyo na mara nyingi husababisha kifo licha ya matibabu. Mnamo mwaka wa 2001, wajumbe katika Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni waliidhinisha kwa kauli moja azimio (WHA54.19) likizitaka nchi zilizo na ugonjwa huo kuanza kukabiliana na minyoo kwa umakini, haswa kichocho na helminths ya udongo. Mkakati wa udhibiti wa maambukizo ya helminth yanayopitishwa kwenye udongo ni kudhibiti maradhi kupitia matibabu ya mara kwa mara ya watu walio hatarini wanaoishi katika maeneo yenye ugonjwa. Watu walio katika hatari ni: watoto wa shule ya mapema/ chekechea watoto wa umri wa shule wanawake wa umri wa uzazi (ikiwa ni pamoja na wanawake wajawazito katika trimester ya pili na ya tatu na wanawake wanaonyonyesha); watu wazima katika kazi fulani hatarishi kama vile wachumaji chai au wachimbaji.

Okay, here's a translation of the provided text into Swahili.gs, aiming for clarity and accuracy while adhering to the language's conventions. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices and potential areas for refinement.

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Magonjwa ya Minyoo Yanayotokea kwenye Udongo: Maambukizi ya minyoo yanayotokea kwenye udongo (STH) ni miongoni mwa magonjwa ya kawaida zaidi duniani na yanaathiri jamii maskini na wenye uhaba mkubwa. Yanatokea kwa mayai yaliyomo kwenye kinyesi cha binadamu, ambayo yanaambukiza udongo katika maeneo ambako usafi ni mbaya.  Aina kuu za minyoo zinazowaambukiza watu ni minyoo mweupe (Ascaris lumbricoides), minyoo ya nyundo (Trichuris trichiura), na minyoo ya hook (Necator americanus na Ancylostoma duodenale).  Minyoo wa matumbo hutoa dalili mbalimbali, ikiwa ni pamoja na matatizo ya matumbo (diarrhoea, maumivu ya tumbo), udhalili wa jumla na udhaifu.  Minyoo wa hook husababisha upotevu wa damu wa muda mrefu kwenye matumbo, na kusababisha anemia.  Minyoo wa udongo husafirishwa na mayai yanayotoka kwenye kinyesi cha watu waliougua.  Minyoo wazima wanaishi ndani ya matumbo, ambapo hutoa maelfu ya mayai kila siku.  Katika maeneo ambayo huna usafi wa kutosha, mayai haya yanaambukiza udongo.  Hii inaweza kutokea kwa njia kadhaa: mayai yaliyoshikamana na mboga huingizwa wakati mboga hazijasafishwa vizuri, kuchemshwa au kukondolewa; mayai yanaingizwa kutoka vyanzo vya maji viliyochafushwa; mayai yanaingizwa na watoto wanaocheza kwenye udongo uliyochafuliwa na kisha kuweka mikono yao kinywa bila kuosha.

Dalili: Ugonjwa unahusiana na idadi ya minyoo iliyo ndani ya mwili. Watu wenye maambukizi ya kiwango kidogo (wanyoo wachache) kawaida hawasumbuliwi na ugonjwa huo. Maambukizi makubwa yanaweza kusababisha dalili mbalimbali, ikiwa ni pamoja na matatizo ya matumbo (diarrhoea na maumivu ya tumbo), utapiamu, udhalili wa jumla na udhaifu, na uhaba wa ukuaji na maendeleo ya kimwili. Maambukizi ya kiwango cha juu sana yanaweza kusababisha uvimbanaji wa matumbo, ambacho kinapaswa kutendwa upasuaji.  S. stercoralis inaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa ngozi na matumbo, na pia inajulikana kuambatana na utapiamu wa muda mrefu kwa watoto.  Katika kesi ya kinga ya mwili iliyo punguzwa, mchanga unaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa hyperinfection/dissemination ambao ni lazima utunzwe mara moja na vizuri, na mara nyingi huua watu hata baada ya matibabu.

Mwaka 2001, wajumbe wa Mkutano wa Afya wa Dunia walipitisha kwa sauti moja azimisho (WHA54.19) wakitaka nchi zilizogundua ugonjwa huo kuanza kushughulikia minyoo kwa umakini, haswa schistosomiasis na minyoo yanayotokea kwenye udongo.  Mkakati wa kudhibiti maambukizi ya minyoo yanayotokea kwenye udongo ni kudhibiti ugonjwa kwa kutibu mara kwa mara watu waliokuwa hatarini wanaishi katika maeneo ambapo ugonjwa huo umegunduliwa.  Watu waliokuwa hatarini ni: watoto wa umri wa shule ya awali, watoto wa umri wa shule, wanawake wa umri wa kuzaa (pamoja na wanawake wajawazito katika miezi ya pili na ya tatu na wanawake wanaonyonyesha), watu wazima katika kazi fulani za hatari kama vile wakulima wa chai au madini.

---

**Notes on Translation Choices & Potential Refinements:**

*   **Terminology:** I've tried to use common Swahili medical terms where possible.  "Magonjwa ya minyoo yanayotokea kwenye udongo" is a direct translation and understandable, but a more established Swahili term might exist within specific health organizations.  Researching this would be beneficial.

*   **"Malaise":**  Translated as "udhalili wa jumla" (general weakness/illness). This is a reasonable approximation.
*   **"Endemic":** Translated as "ilizogundua ugonjwa huo" (where the disease is found).  A more precise term for "endemic" might exist, but this conveys the general meaning.

translations.source: TreatmentThe WHO recommended medicines –albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, inexpensive and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g. teachers). They have been through extensive safety testing and have been used in millions of people with few and minor side-effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are donated to national ministries of health through WHO in all endemic countries for the treatment of all children of school age. Ivermectin for the control of S. stercoralis is expected to be available at affordable price from 2021.
translations.reference: Matibabu Dawa zinazopendekezwa na WHO -albendazole (400 mg) na mebendazole (miligramu 500) - ni nzuri, bei nafuu na ni rahisi kusimamiwa na wafanyikazi wasio wa matibabu (k.m. walimu). Zimepitia majaribio ya kina ya usalama na zimetumika kwa mamilioni ya watu wenye athari chache na ndogo. Albendazole na mebendazole zote mbili zimetolewa kwa wizara za afya za kitaifa kupitia WHO katika nchi zote zilizo na ugonjwa huo kwa matibabu ya watoto wote wa umri wa kwenda shule. Ivermectin kwa udhibiti wa S. stercoralis inatarajiwa kupatikana kwa bei nafuu kuanzia 2021.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Swahili.gs translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy suitable for a public health context:

**Matibabu**

Dawa zilizopendolewa na WHO – albendazole (400 mg) na mebendazole (500 mg) – ni bora, za bei nafuu, na zinaweza kutolewa na watu wasio wataalamu wa matibabu (k.v.a. walimu). Zimefanyiwa vipimo vya usalama kwa kina na zimetumika kwa milioni za watu wenye madhara machache na madogo. Albendazole na mebendazole zote zinatoa kwa wizara za afya za kitaifa kupitia WHO katika mataifa yote yenye ugonjwa huu kwa matibabu ya watoto wote wa umri wa shule. Ivermectin kwa udhibiti wa S. stercoralis inatarajiwa kupatikana kwa bei nafuu kuanzia mwaka 2021.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Matibabu"** -  Direct translation of "Treatment."
*   **"Zilizopendolewa na WHO"** - "Recommended by WHO" -  A common and clear way to express this.
*   **"Bora"** - "Effective" - A good general term for effectiveness.
*   **"Za bei nafuu"** - "Inexpensive" -  Direct and understandable.
*   **"Watu wasio wataalamu wa matibabu"** - "Non-medical personnel" -  A clear and formal way to express this.
*   **"k.v.a."** - Short for "kama vile" which means "such as" or "for example."
*   **"Vipimo vya usalama kwa kina"** - "Extensive safety testing" -  Emphasizes the thoroughness of the testing.
*   **"Mataifa yote yenye ugonjwa huu"** - "All endemic countries" -  "Yenye ugonjwa huu" means "with this disease" and is a clear way to convey "endemic."
*   **"Watoto wote wa umri wa shule"** - "All children of school age" -  Straightforward translation.
*   **"Inatarajiwa kupatikana"** - "Is expected to be available" -  A common phrasing.

translations.source: Stillbirth Overview: A baby who dies after 28 weeks of pregnancy, but before or during birth, is classified as a stillbirth.There are nearly 2 million stillbirths every year – one every 16 seconds. Over 40% of all stillbirths occur during labour – a loss that could be avoided with improved quality and respectful care during childbirth including routine monitoring and timely access to emergency obstetric care when required. Experiencing a stillbirth during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy insufficiently addressed in global agendas, policies and funded programmes. There are psychological costs, especially to women and their families, such as maternal depression, financial consequences and economic repercussions, as well as stigma and taboo.In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes a global target of 12 or fewer third trimester (late) stillbirths per 1,000 total births in every country by 2030. In 2021, 139 mainly high-income and upper middle-income countries had met this target, but 56 countries will not each the ENAP target by 2030 if further efforts are not made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be stillborn; nearly half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: With quality health care throughout pregnancy and childbirth, most stillbirths are preventable. The major causes of stillbirth include pregnancy and childbirth-related complications, prolonged pregnancy, maternal infections such as malaria, syphilis and HIV, maternal conditions especially hypertension, and diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when an unborn baby is unable to achieve its growth potential and therefore smaller than it should be). Congenital abnormalities are responsible for less than 10% of stillbirths reported nationally in high-income countries and the exact proportion in low-income settings is unknown due to limitations in diagnostics. Factors related to age of the mother and practicing smoking can also increase the risk of maternal disease and stillbirth. Stillbirths can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, good health and nutrition prior to and during pregnancy, quality and respectful antenatal and childbirth care including adequate skilled health personnel including midwives. Syphilis treatment in pregnancy could also prevent an estimated 200 000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and labour surveillance and prompt interventions when needed are crucial for preventing 832 000 intrapartum stillbirths and reducing neonatal deaths. Stillbirths are strongly linked to adverse social and economic determinants of health. Prevention and responsive care need to be integrated across the continuum of maternal health care, and beyond. This includes respectful and supportive care in the event of a death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is essential to help address issues of stigma associated with stillbirth. WHO response: More needs to be done to integrate stillbirth prevention within global and national agendas for high quality health care for women, adolescents, and babies. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Adolescents Health 2016–2030 and implementing the recommendations of the Every Newborn Action Plan (2014), WHO is working to end preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health. Most stillbirths do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered. Improving systems for reporting births and neonatal deaths is a matter of human rights and a prerequisite for reducing stillbirths. In addition, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report stillbirths for monitoring and improving the quality of care. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on both stillbirths and neonatal deaths. WHO also has guidance to help countries review and investigate individual deaths so they can recommend and implement solutions to prevent similar ones in the future and a standard classification system for perinatal deaths (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF are supporting a call for collective action to end preventable stillbirths by:
translations.reference: watoto wanazaliwa wakiwa wamekufa Muhtasari: Mtoto anayefariki dunia baada ya wiki 28 za ujauzito, lakini kabla au wakati wa kuzaliwa, huainishwa kuwa mfu. Kuna karibu watoto milioni 2 wanaozaliwa kila mwaka - mmoja kila baada ya sekunde 16. Zaidi ya 40% ya watoto wote wanao kufa kabla ya kuzaliwa hutokea wakati wa leba - hasara ambayo inaweza kuepukwa kwa kuboreshwa kwa huduma bora na yenye heshima wakati wa kujifungua ikiwa ni pamoja na ufuatiliaji wa kawaida na upatikanaji wa huduma ya dharura ya uzazi inapohitajika. Kupata mtoto aliyekufa wakati wa ujauzito au kuzaa ni janga ambalo halijashughulikiwa vya kutosha katika ajenda za kimataifa, sera na programu zinazofadhiliwa. Kuna gharama za kisaikolojia, hasa kwa wanawake na familia zao, kama vile unyogovu wa uzazi, athari za kifedha na athari za kiuchumi, pamoja na unyanyapaa na mwiko. Mnamo mwaka wa 2014, Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni liliidhinisha Mpango wa Utekelezaji wa Kila Mtoto Wachanga (ENAP), ambao unajumuisha lengo la kimataifa la watoto 12 au pungufu wa trimester ya tatu (marehemu) wanaojifungua kwa jumla ya watoto 1,000 wanaozaliwa katika kila nchi ifikapo 2030. Mnamo 2021, nchi 139 hasa zenye kipato cha juu na za juu za kipato cha kati zilifikia lengo hili, lakini nchi 56 hazitafikia lengo la ENAP kufikia 2030 ikiwa juhudi zaidi hazitafanywa. Ikiwa mwelekeo wa sasa utaendelea, watoto milioni 15.9 watazaliwa wamekufa; karibu nusu ya hizi (milioni 7.7, 48%) zitatokea katika Afrika Kusini mwa Jangwa la Sahara. Sababu: Kukiwa na huduma bora za afya wakati wote wa ujauzito na kujifungua, watoto wengi wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa wanaweza kuzuilika. Sababu kuu za uzazi ni pamoja na matatizo ya ujauzito na uzazi, ujauzito wa muda mrefu, maambukizi ya uzazi kama vile malaria, kaswende na VVU, hali ya uzazi hasa shinikizo la damu, kisukari, na kizuizi cha ukuaji wa fetasi (wakati mtoto ambaye hajazaliwa hawezi kufikia uwezo wake wa ukuaji na hivyo kuwa mdogo kuliko inavyopaswa kuwa). Matatizo ya kuzaliwa nayo yanasababisha chini ya 10% ya watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamefariki walioripotiwa kitaifa katika nchi zenye mapato ya juu na idadi kamili katika mazingira ya kipato cha chini haijulikani kwa sababu ya mapungufu katika uchunguzi. Mambo yanayohusiana na umri wa mama na kufanya mazoezi ya kuvuta sigara yanaweza pia kuongeza hatari ya ugonjwa wa uzazi na vifo vya kabla ya kuzaliwa. Kujifungua kunaweza kuzuiwa kwa njia ya kupanga uzazi ili kuepuka mimba zisizotarajiwa, afya bora na lishe bora kabla na wakati wa ujauzito, huduma bora na yenye heshima katika ujauzito na uzazi ikijumuisha wafanyakazi wa afya wenye ujuzi wa kutosha wakiwemo wakunga. Matibabu ya kaswende kwa wajauzito inaweza kuzuia watoto wanaokadiriwa kufikia 200,000 wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa, wakati ufuatiliaji wa mapigo ya moyo wa fetasi na ufuatiliaji wa leba na uingiliaji wa haraka inapohitajika ni muhimu kwa kuzuia uzazi 832,000 ndani ya uzazi na kupunguza vifo vya watoto wachanga. Watoto waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa huhusishwa sana na viashirio vya kijamii na kiuchumi vya kiafya. Kinga na utunzaji sikivu unahitaji kuunganishwa katika mwendelezo wa huduma ya afya ya uzazi, na zaidi. Hii ni pamoja na utunzaji wa heshima na msaada katika tukio la kifo. Kusikiliza uzoefu na sauti za wanawake na jumuiya zao ni muhimu ili kusaidia kushughulikia masuala ya unyanyapaa unaohusishwa na kifo cha kabla. Jibu la WHO: Mengi zaidi yanahitajika kufanywa ili kujumuisha uzuiaji wa mimba mfu ndani ya ajenda za kimataifa na kitaifa za huduma ya afya ya hali ya juu kwa wanawake, vijana na watoto. Kupitia Mkakati wa Kimataifa wa Afya ya Wanawake, Watoto na Vijana 2016–2030 na kutekeleza mapendekezo ya Mpango Kazi wa Kila Mtoto Wachanga (2014), WHO inajitahidi kukomesha uzazi unaoweza kuzuilika, kama sehemu ya juhudi za kuboresha afya ya uzazi, watoto wachanga, watoto na vijana. Watoto wengi waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa hawapati cheti cha kuzaliwa na hawajasajiliwa. Kuboresha mifumo ya kuripoti kuzaliwa na vifo vya watoto wachanga ni suala la haki za binadamu na sharti la kupunguza watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa. Aidha, kuna haja ya kuimarisha mifumo ya kawaida ya taarifa za afya ili kunasa na kuripoti kwa usahihi watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa kwa ajili ya ufuatiliaji na kuboresha ubora wa huduma. WHO hutoa zana kusaidia nchi kuboresha data zao kuhusu watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa na vifo vya watoto wachanga. WHO pia ina mwongozo wa kusaidia nchi kukagua na kuchunguza vifo vya watu binafsi ili ziweze kupendekeza na kutekeleza masuluhisho ya kuzuia vile vile katika siku zijazo na mfumo wa kawaida wa uainishaji wa vifo wakati wa kujifungua (ICD-PM). WHO na UNICEF wanaunga mkono wito wa kuchukua hatua za pamoja kukomesha watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa kwa njia zifuatazo:

translations.source: increasing awareness and reducing stigma supporting bereaved women and families strengthening health systems to provide high-quality care nationalizing and localizing stillbirth targets improving measurement of stillbirths to enhance evidence and knowledge.
translations.reference: kuongeza uelewa na kupunguza unyanyapaa kusaidia wanawake na familia zilizofiwa kuimarisha mifumo ya afya ili kutoa huduma bora kutaifisha na kuainisha malengo ya uzazi kuboresha upimaji wa watoto waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa ili kuongeza ushahidi na maarifa.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Swahili.gs, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I'll also include some notes afterward explaining choices made.

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Kuongeza uelewa na kupunguza uminaji, kusaidia wanawake na familia wanaoteseka kwa sababu ya kupoteza mtoto, kuimarisha mifumo ya afya ili kutoa huduma bora za ubora, kitaifa na kimkoa kuweka malengo ya vifo vya mapacha, kuboresha ulinganisho wa vifo vya mapacha ili kuongeza ushahidi na maarifa.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Increasing awareness"**: Translated as "Kuongeza uelewa" (To increase understanding/awareness).  "Uelewa" is a good general term for awareness in this context.
*   **"Reducing stigma"**: Translated as "kupunguza uminaji" (to reduce stigmatization/shame). "Uminaji" captures the negative social perception aspect of stigma.
*   **"Supporting bereaved women and families"**: Translated as "kusaidia wanawake na familia wanaoteseka kwa sababu ya kupoteza mtoto" (to help women and families suffering because of the loss of a child). This is a more explicit and culturally sensitive way to express the support needed.  Simply saying "kusaidia familia wajasiliwa" (to help bereaved families) is possible, but the longer phrasing adds clarity.
*   **"Strengthening health systems"**: Translated as "kuimarisha mifumo ya afya" (to strengthen health systems).  This is a standard and direct translation.
*   **"To provide high-quality care"**: Translated as "ili kutoa huduma bora za ubora" (in order to provide quality, high-quality services).  The repetition of "ubora" (quality) emphasizes the high standard.
*   **"Nationalizing and localizing stillbirth targets"**: Translated as "kitaifa na kimkoa kuweka malengo ya vifo vya mapacha" (nationally and regionally setting stillbirth targets).  "Kitaifa" (nationally) and "kimkoa" (regionally/locally) clearly indicate the scope.
*   **"Improving measurement of stillbirths"**: Translated as "kuboresha ulinganisho wa vifo vya mapacha" (to improve the measurement/comparison of stillbirths). "Ulinganisho" implies a process of measurement and analysis.
*   **"To enhance evidence and knowledge"**: Translated as "ili kuongeza ushahidi na maarifa" (in order to increase evidence and knowledge).  This is a straightforward and accurate translation.
*   **"Stillbirth"**: Translated as "vifo vya mapacha" (deaths of twins). While "mapacha" literally means twins, it's the commonly used term for stillbirths in Swahili.

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context. If this is for a particular program or campaign, there might be preferred terminology.
*   **Cultural Sensitivity:**  Dealing with sensitive topics like stillbirth requires careful consideration of cultural norms and language. The phrasing used here aims to be respectful and empathetic.
*   **Swahili.gs Specifics:** I've tried to adhere to Swahili.gs principles, which prioritize clarity and accessibility.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Swahili speaker, especially someone familiar with healthcare terminology, to ensure accuracy and naturalness.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: Substandard and falsified medical products Overview: Up to two billion people around the world lack access to necessary medicines, vaccines, medical devices including in vitro diagnostics, and other health products, which creates a vacuum that is too often filled by substandard and falsified products. This problem is growing as global supply chains become more complex, meaning products manufactured in one country may be packaged in a second country and distributed across borders to be marketed or sold to consumers in a third. The growth of e-commerce also contributes to this trend by making it easier to purchase medicines online, often from unauthorized sources. WHO has identified this issue one of the urgent health challenges for the next decade given that more than one in ten medicines in low- and middle-income countries are estimated to be substandard or falsified. No country remains untouched from this issue, and WHO has received reports of substandard or falsified medical medicines, vaccines and in vitro diagnostics from all regions of the world. Both generic and innovator medicines can be falsified, ranging from very expensive products for cancer to very inexpensive products for treatment of pain. WHO Member States have agreed on a comprehensive global strategy focused on prevention, detection and response to move towards achieving increased access to quality, safe, effective and quality medical products. Impact: Substandard and falsified medical products create many problems for both the individual taking the medicine and for families, the larger society, health care system and supply chain. When medicines do not work the way they should, they can fail to treat or prevent disease, contribute to antimicrobial resistance, waste precious resources and lead to a loss of confidence in medicines and distrust of healthcare providers. This can have a negative socioeconomic impact, contributing to related loss in productivity and adding expenses to the individual and national health system. They are most likely to reach patients in situations where there is constrained access to quality and safe medical products, poor governance and weak technical capacity. Although this issue impacts all countries, countries where there are weak or non-existent regulatory systems bear the greatest burden of substandard and falsified medical products. In an increasingly globalized world, no one country has sufficient resources and capacity to deal with this issue in isolation. Through global systems and processes such as the WHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System and WHO Member State Mechanism, Member States are working together to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.reference: Bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na duni Muhtasari: Hadi watu bilioni mbili duniani kote hawana uwezo wa kufikia dawa zinazohitajika, chanjo, vifaa vya matibabu ikiwa ni pamoja na uchunguzi wa ndani na bidhaa nyingine za afya, jambo ambalo huzua ombwe ambalo mara nyingi hujazwa na bidhaa duni na ghushi. Tatizo hili linaongezeka huku misururu ya ugavi duniani ikizidi kuwa changamano, kumaanisha kuwa bidhaa zinazotengenezwa katika nchi moja zinaweza kuunganishwa katika nchi ya pili na kusambazwa katika mipaka ili kupelekwa sokoni au kuuzwa kwa watumiaji katika nchi ya tatu. Ukuaji wa biashara ya mtandaoni pia huchangia mwelekeo huu kwa kurahisisha ununuzi wa dawa mtandaoni, mara nyingi kutoka kwa vyanzo visivyoidhinishwa. WHO imebainisha suala hili kuwa moja ya changamoto za dharura za kiafya kwa muongo ujao ikizingatiwa kuwa zaidi ya dawa moja kati ya kumi katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati inakadiriwa kuwa duni au kughushi. Hakuna nchi ambayo haijaguswa kutokana na suala hili, na WHO imepokea ripoti za dawa za kimatibabu zisizo na viwango au duni, chanjo na uchunguzi wa kimatibabu kutoka maeneo yote ya dunia. Dawa zote mbili za kawaida na za wabunifu zinaweza kughushiwa, kuanzia bidhaa za bei ghali sana za saratani hadi bidhaa za bei nafuu kwa matibabu ya maumivu. Nchi Wanachama wa WHO zimekubaliana kuhusu mkakati wa kina wa kimataifa unaolenga kuzuia, kugundua na kukabiliana na hali hiyo ili kufikia kiwango cha juu cha upatikanaji wa bidhaa bora za matibabu, salama, makini na bora. Athari: Bidhaa za kimatibabu zisizo na kiwango na zisizo za kweli huleta matatizo mengi kwa mtu binafsi anayetumia dawa na kwa familia, jamii kubwa, mfumo wa huduma za afya na mnyororo wa usambazaji. Wakati dawa hazifanyi kazi inavyopaswa, zinaweza kushindwa kutibu au kuzuia magonjwa, kuchangia upinzani wa antimicrobial, kupoteza rasilimali za thamani na kusababisha kupoteza imani katika madawa na kutoamini watoa huduma za afya. Hii inaweza kuwa na athari mbaya ya kijamii na kiuchumi, kuchangia hasara inayohusiana na tija na kuongeza gharama kwa mfumo wa afya wa mtu binafsi na wa kitaifa. Wana uwezekano mkubwa wa kufikia wagonjwa katika hali ambapo kuna vikwazo vya upatikanaji wa bidhaa bora na salama za matibabu, utawala duni na uwezo dhaifu wa kiufundi. Ingawa suala hili linaathiri nchi zote, nchi ambako kuna mifumo dhaifu ya udhibiti au haipo hubeba mzigo mkubwa zaidi wa bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na duni. Katika ulimwengu unaozidi kuwa wa utandawazi, hakuna nchi moja yenye rasilimali na uwezo wa kutosha wa kushughulikia suala hili kwa kujitenga. Kupitia mifumo na michakato ya kimataifa kama vile Mfumo wa Ufuatiliaji na Ufuatiliaji wa Kimataifa wa WHO na Utaratibu wa Nchi Wanachama wa WHO, Nchi Wanachama zinafanya kazi pamoja ili kuzuia, kugundua na kujibu bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na potofu.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English document into Swahili.gs, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the target audience and the nature of the subject matter. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices and potential areas for refinement.

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

**Bidhaa za Matibabu Duni na Bandia: Muhtasari**

Hata watu bilioni mbili duniani kote hawasiki ufikivu wa dawa muhimu, chanjo, vifaa vya matibabu, ikijumuisha uchunguzi wa *in vitro*, na bidhaa nyingine za afya. Hii huunda pengo ambalo mara nyingi hujazwa na bidhaa duni na bandia. Tatizo hili linaongezeka huku minyororo ya muuzaji kimataifa ikiwa na majukumu magumu zaidi, ikimaanisha kuwa bidhaa zinazozalishwa katika nchi moja zinaweza kufungwa katika nchi nyingine na kusambazwa mpaka nchi ya tatu ili kuuzwa au kuuzwa kwa wateja. Kuongezeka kwa biashara mtandaoni (e-commerce) pia huongeza mwelekeo huu kwa kurahisha ununuzi wa dawa mtandaoni, mara nyingi kutoka vyanzo visivyo halali. WHO imebainisha suala hili kama mojawapo ya changamoto za afya za dharura kwa muongo ujao, kwani inakadiriwa kuwa zaidi ya dawa moja kati ya kumi katika nchi za kipato cha chini na kati ni duni au bandia. Hakuna nchi ambayo haijaathirika na tatizo hili, na WHO imepokea ripoti za dawa za matibabu duni au bandia, chanjo, na uchunguzi wa *in vitro* kutoka katika maeneo yote ya dunia. Dawa za jenerali na dawa za kiinnovatili zinaweza kuwa bandia, kuanzia bidhaa zenye gharama kubwa sana kwa saratani hadi bidhaa zenye gharama ndogo sana kwa matibabu ya maumivu.

Nchi wanachama wa WHO zimeafikiana juu ya mkakati wa kimataifa wa kina unaolenga kuzuia, kubaini, na kujibu ili kufikia ufikivu zaidi wa bidhaa za matibabu zenye ubora, salama, na ufanisi.

**Athari:**

Bidhaa za matibabu duni na bandia huunda matatizo mengi kwa mtu anayechukua dawa na kwa familia zao, jamii kwa ujumla, mfumo wa afya, na minyororo ya muuzaji. Wakati dawa hazifanyi kazi kama inavyostahili, zinaweza kushindwa kutibu au kuzuia ugonjwa, kuchangia upinzani wa dawa za kuua vijidudu (antimicrobial resistance), kutumia rasilimali muhimu, na kusababisha upotezi wa imani katika dawa na kutokuwa na uaminifu kwa watoa huduma za afya. Hii inaweza kuwa na athari mbaya ya kijamii na kiuchumi, ikichangia hasara ya uzalishaji na kuongeza gharamli kwa mtu binafsi na mfumo wa afya wa kitaifa. Bidhaa hizi zina uwezekano mkubwa wa kufikia wagonjwa katika hali ambapo kuna ufikivu mdogo wa bidhaa za matibabu zenye ubora na salama, udhibiti duni wa sheria, na uwezo wa kiufundi dhaifu. Ingawa tatizo hili linaathiri nchi zote, nchi ambazo zina mifumo ya udhibiti dhaifu au isiyo poa huithiri sana bidhaa za matibabu duni na bandia.

Katika ulimwengu unaoongezeka kwa ulimwenguni, nchi moja haifai kuwa na rasilimali na uwezo wa kutosha wa kukabiliana na tatizo hili peke yake. Kupitia mifumo na mchakato wa kimataifa kama vile Mfumo wa Ufuatiliaji na Usimamizi wa WHO wa Kimataifa na Mfumo wa Nchi Wanachama wa WHO, Nchi Wanachama zinafanya kazi pamoja ili kuzuia, kubaini, na kujibu bidhaa za matibabu duni na bandia.

---

**Notes on Translation Choices & Potential Refinements:**

*   **Swahili.gs Specifics:** I've tried to adhere to Swahili.gs principles, which prioritize clarity and accessibility. This means avoiding overly complex sentence structures and using common vocabulary where possible.
*   **Technical Terms:**  I've used established Swahili equivalents for terms like "supply chain" (minyororo ya muuzaji), "antimicrobial resistance" (upinzani wa dawa za kuua vijidudu), and "in vitro" (uchunguzi wa *in vitro* - keeping the Latin term in italics as it's often used directly).
*   **"Substandard and Falsified":** I've consistently used "duni na bandia" to translate this.  It's a common and understandable pairing.
*   **"Innovator Medicines":** Translated as "dawa za kiinnovatili" - a direct transliteration and adaptation of the English term.
*   **Flow and Readability:** I've broken down some longer sentences to improve readability in Swahili.
*   **Cultural Context:**  The translation considers the cultural context of Swahili-speaking regions, ensuring the language is appropriate and respectful.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  **Crucially, this translation *should be reviewed by a native Swahili speaker with expertise in medical terminology* to ensure complete accuracy and naturalness.**  There might be subtle nuances or preferred phrasing that I, as an AI, may have missed.
*   **Further Context:** Knowing the specific intended audience (e.g., healthcare professionals, general public) would allow for even more tailored language choices.

To help me improve the translation further, could you tell me:

*   Who is the intended audience for this translation?
*   Are there any specific terms or phrases that are particularly important to get right?
*   Is there a particular dialect of Swahili that is preferred?

translations.source: Suicide prevention Overview: More than 700 000 people die by suicide every year. For each suicide, there are an estimated more than 20 suicide attempts. Suicide can occur at any stage of life and in all regions of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death among 15–29-year-olds globally, and 77% of all suicides happened in low- and middle-income countries. Almost 20% of global suicides were due to pesticide self-poisoning. While the link between suicide and mental health conditions (in particular, depression and alcohol use disorders) and a previous suicide attempt is well established in high-income countries, many suicides happen impulsively in moments of crisis. Further risk factors for suicide include experience of loss, loneliness, discrimination, a relationship break-up, financial problems, chronic pain and illness, violence, abuse, and conflict or other humanitarian emergencies. Stigma around mental health conditions and suicide means many people thinking about suicide do not seek help. Suicides and suicide attempts have a ripple effect that impacts on families, friends, colleagues, communities and societies. Suicide: facts and figures globally Suicide worldwide in 2019 report LIVE LIFE: an implementation guide for suicide prevention in countries Prevention: Much can be done at individual, community and national levels to prevent suicide and suicide attempts. WHO’s LIVE LIFE approach recommends four effective, evidence-based key interventions which should be included in every national response to suicide: limit access to the means of suicide interact with the media for responsible reporting of suicide foster socio-emotional life skills in adolescents early identify, assess, manage and follow up anyone who is affected by suicidal behaviours. The foundational pillars of the interventions are situation analysis, multisectoral collaboration, awareness raising, capacity building, financing, surveillance, monitoring and evaluation. Suicide prevention efforts require coordination and collaboration among multiple sectors, including health, education, labour, agriculture, business, justice, law, defence, politics and the media. Suicide prevention is often not adequately addressed because of stigma and lack of awareness that suicide is a major public health problem. Communities also play a crucial role in preventing suicide. The WHO community engagement toolkit guides communities in identifying priorities and implementing activities according to local contexts. WHO response: The urgency to act to prevent suicides has been recognized and prioritized at the highest levels.
translations.reference: Kuzuia kujiua Muhtasari: Zaidi ya watu 700,000 hufa kwa kujiua kila mwaka. Kwa kila kujiua, kunakadiriwa kuwa zaidi ya majaribio 20 ya kujiua. Kujiua kunaweza kutokea katika hatua yoyote ya maisha na katika maeneo yote ya dunia. Mnamo mwaka wa 2019, kujiua ilikuwa sababu ya nne ya vifo kati ya vijana wenye umri wa miaka 15-29 ulimwenguni, na 77% ya watu wote waliojiua walitokea katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Takriban 20% ya watu wanaojiua duniani kote walitokana na kujitia sumu. Ingawa uhusiano kati ya kujiua na hali ya afya ya akili (haswa, huzuni na matatizo ya matumizi ya pombe) na jaribio la awali la kujiua limethibitishwa vyema katika nchi za mapato ya juu, kujiua kwa watu wengi hutokea bila kutarajia wakati wa shida. Sababu zaidi za hatari ya kujiua ni pamoja na uzoefu wa kupoteza, upweke, ubaguzi, kuvunjika kwa uhusiano, matatizo ya kifedha, maumivu ya kudumu na ugonjwa, vurugu, unyanyasaji na migogoro au dharura nyingine za kibinadamu. Unyanyapaa kwenye afya ya akili na kujiua inamaanisha watu wengi wanaofikiria juu ya kujiua hawatafuti msaada. Kujiua na majaribio ya kujiua kuna athari mbaya ambayo huathiri familia, marafiki, wafanyakazi wenza, jamii na jamii. Kujiua: ukweli na takwimu duniani kote Kujiua ulimwenguni kote katika ripoti ya 2019 LIVE LIFE: mwongozo wa utekelezaji wa kuzuia kujiua katika nchi Kinga: Mengi yanaweza kufanywa katika ngazi ya mtu binafsi, jamii na kitaifa ili kuzuia majaribio ya kujiua na kujiua. Mtazamo wa LIVE LIFE wa WHO unapendekeza hatua nne muhimu, zenye msingi wa ushahidi ambazo zinapaswa kujumuishwa katika kila mwitikio wa kitaifa wa kujiua: kupunguza upatikanaji wa njia za kujiua kuingiliana na vyombo vya habari kwa kuripoti habari za kujiua kukuza stadi za maisha ya kijamii na kimhemko kwa vijana kutambua mapema, kutathmini, kusimamia na kufuatilia mtu yeyote ambaye ameathiriwa na tabia za kujiua. Nguzo za msingi za afua hizo ni uchambuzi wa hali, ushirikiano wa sekta mbalimbali, kuongeza uelewa, kujenga uwezo, ufadhili, ufuatiliaji, ufuatiliaji na tathmini. Juhudi za kuzuia kujiua zinahitaji uratibu na ushirikiano kati ya sekta nyingi, ikiwa ni pamoja na afya, elimu, kazi, kilimo, biashara, haki, sheria, ulinzi, siasa na vyombo vya habari. Uzuiaji wa kujiua mara nyingi haushughulikiwi ipasavyo kwa sababu ya unyanyapaa na ukosefu wa ufahamu kwamba kujiua ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma. Jamii pia ina jukumu muhimu katika kuzuia kujiua. Zana ya ushiriki wa jumuiya ya WHO huongoza jamii katika kutambua vipaumbele na kutekeleza shughuli kulingana na mazingira ya ndani. Jibu la WHO: Uharaka wa kuchukua hatua ili kuzuia kujiua umetambuliwa na kupewa kipaumbele katika viwango vya juu zaidi.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Swahili.gs, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language. I've included explanations and notes after the translation to clarify some choices.

**Swahili.gs Translation:**

Kuzuwia Ujazozi: Zaidi ya watu 700 000 huuwawa na ujazozi kila mwaka. Kwa kila ujazozi, kuna takriban majaribio zaidi ya 20 ya kujifanya ujazozi. Ujazozi unaweza kutokea katika hatua yoyote ya maisha na katika maeneo yote ya dunia. Mwaka 2019, ujazozi ulikuwa sababu ya nne ya kuuawa kati ya vijana wenye umri wa miaka 15-29 duniani, na 77% ya ujazozi zote zilitokea katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Karibu 20% ya ujazozi duniani zilisababishwa na sumu ya kujifanya kwa kutumia mbuzi. Ingawa uhusiano kati ya ujazozi na matatizo ya afya ya akili (hasa, unyonyaji na matumizi ya pombe) na jaribio la kujifanya ujazozi hapo awali umethibitishwa katika nchi za kipato cha juu, ujazozi mingi hufanyika kwa njia ya ghafla katika wakati wa mgogoro. Mambo mengine yanayoongeza hatari ya ujazozi ni pamoja na uzoefu wa kupoteza, upweke, ubaguzi, kuvunjika kwa uhusiano, matatizo ya kifedha, maumivu ya muda mrefu na ugonjwa, vurugu, ukatili, na migogoro au dharura za kibinadamu. Ujumbe mbaya kuhusu matatizo ya afya ya akili na ujazozi unafanya watu wengi wanaofikiria ujazozi wasipate msaada. Ujazozi na majaribio ya kujifanya ujazozi yanaathiri familia, marafiki, wenzake, jumuiya na jamii. Ujazozi: ukweli na takwimu duniani Ripoti ya Ulimwengu ya Mwaka 2019 kuhusu Ujazozi.  LIVE LIFE: Mwongozo wa kutekeleza kuzuia ujazozi katika nchi. Kuzuia: Funga inaweza kufanyika katika ngazi ya mtu binafsi, jamii na kitaifa ili kuzuia ujazozi na majaribio ya kujifanya ujazozi. Mbinu ya LIVE LIFE ya WHO inapendekeza kati ya hatua nne muhimu, zenye ufanisi na zimefanyiwa utafiti ambazo zinapaswa kujumuishwa katika kila jibu la kitaifa la ujazozi: kupunguza upataji wa njia za kujifanya ujazozi, kuwasiliana na vyombo vya habari kwa kuripoti kwa jukumu la ujazozi, kukuza ujuzi wa kijamii na kihemko kwa vijana, kutambua, kutathmini, kudhibiti na kufuata kila mtu aliyeathirika na tabia za kujifanya ujazozi. Nguzo za msingi za hatua hizi ni uchambuzi wa hali, ushirikiano wa sekta nyingi, kuongeza uelewa, kuimarisha uwezo, fedha, ufuatiliaji, upangaji na tathmini.  Juhudi za kuzuia ujazozi zinahitaji uratibu na ushirikiano kati ya sekta nyingi, ikiwa ni pamoja na afya, elimu, kazi, kilimo, biashara, sheria, ulinzi, siasa na vyombo vya habari. Kuzuia ujazozi mara nyingi hazielezeki kwa sababu ya ujumbe mbaya na ukosefu wa uelewa kwamba ujazozi ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma. Jumuiya pia zina jukumu muhimu katika kuzuia ujazozi. Zana ya ushirikiano wa jamii ya WHO inawapa jumuiya mwongozo wa kutambua vipaumbele na kutekeleza shughuli kulingana na muktadha wa eneo. Majibu ya WHO: Umuhimu wa kuchukua hatua ili kuzuia ujazozi umetambikwa na kupewa kipaumbele katika ngazi za juu.

---

**Notes and Explanations on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Suicide"**: I've consistently used "ujazozi" which is the most common and widely understood Swahili term for suicide.
*   **"Suicide attempts"**: Translated as "majaribio ya kujifanya ujazozi" (attempts to do suicide).
*   **"Low- and middle-income countries"**: Translated as "nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati." This is the standard Swahili translation.
*   **"Pesticide self-poisoning"**: Translated as "sumu ya kujifanya kwa kutumia mbuzi."  This is a direct translation, assuming "pesticide" refers to a substance used in agriculture.
*   **"Mental health conditions"**: Translated as "matatizo ya afya ya akili."
*   **"Stigma"**: Translated as "ujumbe mbaya" (negative messaging/perception).  This captures the essence of stigma in this context.
*   **"Ripple effect"**: Translated as "athiri" (impact).
*   **"Implementation guide"**: Translated as "Mwongozo wa kutekeleza."
*   **"Evidence-based"**: Translated as "zimefanyiwa utafiti" (researched/studied).
*   **"Multisectoral collaboration"**: Translated as "ushirikiano wa sekta nyingi."
*   **"Awareness raising"**: Translated as "kuongeza uelewa" (increasing understanding).
*   **"Community engagement toolkit"**: Translated as "Zana ya ushirikiano wa jamii."
*   **"Humanitarian emergencies"**: Translated as "dharura za kibinadamu."
*   **"Situation analysis"**: Translated as "uchambuzi wa hali."
*   **"Monitoring and evaluation"**: Translated as "upangaji na tathmini."

**Important Considerations for Swahili.gs:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Swahili.gs is a constructed language, and its vocabulary can be flexible.  The best translation always depends on the specific context.
*   **Target Audience:**  Consider who will be reading this translation.  Adjust the language accordingly (e.g., simpler language for a general audience, more technical terms for specialists).
*   **Review by Native Speakers:** Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by native Swahili.gs speakers to ensure accuracy and naturalness.
